ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Continued To Dec-18-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft Hilton To Provide Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Bryce Gray, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Nadia Oude Voshaar
No other appearance.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Nadia Oude Voshaar's Motion To Compel Further Responses is moot in part and granted in part. Supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. While the court does not make an order that objections have been waived, counsel is advised that the supplemental responses made subject to prior objections are not adequate and would not be approved by the court.
The motion is granted as to Request For Admissions Numbers 6 and 8. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Defendant is ordered to respond further. While the response may assert that Defendant is unable to admit or deny, it must be made in compliance with the requirements of the Code Of Civil Procedure.
The motion is granted as to Request For Documents Number 14. Defendant's objections are overruled. Additionally, the response, limiting production to documents relating to this slip and fall, is inadequate.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the late responses and the failure to supplement fully were misuses of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,000, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
